recipes.title: Couscous
recipes.description: Tunisian semolina couscous steamed over a fiery harissa-spiced broth of lamb, vegetables, and chickpeas.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight, then drain.
Brown cubed lamb in olive oil with chopped onions; stir in tomato paste, harissa, turmeric, and caraway.
Add chickpeas, salt, and 2 liters of water; simmer 1 hour.
Moisten couscous with cold salted water, rake with fingers, and steam over the broth in a couscoussier for 20 minutes.
Tip into a tray, drizzle with oil, and rake again to separate; steam another 20 minutes.
Add chunks of carrot, turnip, and potato to the broth; simmer 20 minutes, then add courgette for 10 more.
Steam couscous a final 15 minutes; mound on a platter, top with meat and vegetables, and ladle over the spicy broth.

recipes.title: Brik
recipes.description: Tunisian thin pastry triangle stuffed with egg, tuna, capers, and parsley, deep fried until crackling crisp.
steps: Boil potatoes until tender, then mash with chopped onion, drained tuna, parsley, capers, harissa, and salt.
Place a brik sheet on the work surface, spread a quarter of the filling on one half, and make a well in the center.
Crack a whole egg into the well and immediately fold the pastry into a triangle, sealing edges firmly.
Heat oil to 180C in a deep skillet.
Slide a brik into the oil and baste the top with hot oil for 90 seconds until the pastry is crisp and the white is just set.
Drain briefly on paper and serve immediately with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Shakshouka
recipes.description: Tunisian peppers, tomato, and harissa stew topped with poached eggs, served straight from the pan with crusty bread.
steps: Sauté chopped onion and peppers in olive oil for 10 minutes until soft and beginning to char.
Add minced garlic, cumin, caraway, and harissa; cook 30 seconds.
Stir in chopped tomatoes and salt and simmer 15 minutes until thickened.
Make 6 wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each.
Cover and cook on low heat for 7 minutes until whites are just set.
Scatter chopped parsley and serve from the pan with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Mloukhia
recipes.description: Tunisian dark green stew of dried jute leaves slow-cooked with beef and bay leaves until almost black.
steps: Heat olive oil in a heavy pot and gently toast the dried mloukhia powder for 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
Whisk in 500 ml hot water and stir until smooth without lumps.
Add chopped onion, smashed garlic, bay leaves, tomato paste, coriander, salt, and pepper.
Stir in cubed beef and another 750 ml water and simmer covered on lowest heat for 3 hours.
Stir occasionally and add hot water if too thick; the stew should be very dark and silky.
Adjust seasoning and serve over white rice or with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Ojja
recipes.description: Tunisian skillet of tomato, peppers, and merguez sausage with eggs cracked in, finished with harissa.
steps: Slice merguez and brown in olive oil; remove and set aside.
In the same oil, sauté chopped onion and peppers for 8 minutes.
Add minced garlic, harissa, and caraway; cook 30 seconds.
Stir in chopped tomatoes and salt; simmer 15 minutes until reduced.
Return the merguez to the pan.
Crack eggs directly into the sauce and stir gently until just set but still runny.
Serve straight from the pan with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Lablabi
recipes.description: Tunisian street-food chickpea soup poured over crusty bread with cumin, harissa, lemon, and a soft egg.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight with a pinch of baking soda and salt.
Drain and simmer chickpeas in 2 liters fresh water with smashed garlic for 1.5 hours until very soft.
Stir in cumin, salt, and 1 tablespoon olive oil; the broth should remain thin.
Poach eggs in the simmering broth for 4 minutes.
Tear stale bread into a deep bowl; spoon a teaspoon of harissa over the top.
Ladle the chickpeas and broth over the bread.
Top with a poached egg, drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice, and finish with tuna, capers, and parsley.

recipes.title: Tajine Tunisien
recipes.description: Tunisian baked egg and meat tajine, like a Maghrebi frittata of chicken or lamb, cheese, and parsley.
steps: Brown chopped chicken with sliced onion in olive oil with turmeric, cinnamon, salt, and pepper.
Add 200 ml water and simmer 20 minutes until chicken is tender; shred and reserve any liquid.
Boil cubed potato until tender and drain.
Beat the eggs with breadcrumbs, cheese, and chopped parsley.
Fold in the shredded chicken and potato and any reserved cooking liquid.
Pour into an oiled baking dish and bake at 180C for 35 minutes until set and golden.
Cool slightly, cut into squares, and serve with harissa and lemon.

recipes.title: Salade Méchouia
recipes.description: Tunisian smoky salad of fire-roasted peppers, tomatoes, and garlic chopped together with olive oil, tuna, and capers.
steps: Char peppers, tomatoes, and unpeeled garlic over an open flame or under a hot broiler until blackened all over.
Cool peppers in a covered bowl, then peel, seed, and finely chop.
Peel and chop the charred tomatoes; squeeze the soft garlic from the skins.
Hard-boil the eggs, peel, and quarter.
Combine peppers, tomatoes, and garlic with caraway, coriander, salt, lemon juice, and olive oil.
Spread on a platter and top with flaked tuna, capers, and quartered eggs.

recipes.title: Harissa
recipes.description: Tunisia's signature fiery red paste of dried chilies, garlic, caraway, and coriander, the soul of every Tunisian table.
steps: Stem and seed the dried chilies, then soak in hot water for 30 minutes.
Toast caraway and coriander seeds in a dry pan until fragrant, then grind to a powder.
Drain the chilies and pat dry.
Pound chilies, garlic, ground spices, cumin, and salt in a mortar until smooth, or blend.
Stream in olive oil while blending until thick and glossy.
Pack into a jar, top with a layer of olive oil to seal, and refrigerate up to 1 month.

recipes.title: Kefta
recipes.description: Tunisian spiced minced meat balls simmered in a tomato and harissa sauce, served over couscous or with bread.
steps: Mix minced beef with grated onion, garlic, chopped parsley, half the cumin, salt, and pepper; knead until tacky.
Roll into walnut-sized balls.
Brown the meatballs in olive oil and remove.
In the same pan, sauté chopped onion until soft, then stir in tomato paste, harissa, remaining cumin, and coriander.
Add chopped tomatoes, salt, and 200 ml water; simmer 10 minutes.
Return the meatballs to the sauce and simmer 20 minutes until cooked through and the sauce is thick.
Serve over couscous with extra harissa on the side.

recipes.title: Makroudh
recipes.description: Tunisian semolina diamond-cut pastry stuffed with date paste, fried, and dipped in honey and orange blossom syrup.
steps: Rub semolina with melted ghee and salt until sandy; rest 1 hour.
Add warm water and 1 tablespoon orange blossom water; knead just until it holds, do not over work.
Mash dates with cinnamon and 1 tablespoon orange blossom water into a smooth paste.
Roll the dough into long rectangles; pipe a rope of date paste down the center, fold, and seal.
Cut on the diagonal into small diamonds and press a pattern with a fork.
Fry in 170C oil for 3 minutes until pale gold.
Warm honey with the remaining orange blossom water and dip the hot pastries to coat.
Drain on a rack and let the syrup set before serving with mint tea.

recipes.title: Chorba Frik
recipes.description: Maghrebi soup of cracked freekeh wheat, lamb, tomato, and chickpeas, perfumed with cinnamon and mint for Ramadan.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight, then drain.
Brown cubed lamb in olive oil with chopped onion and celery.
Stir in tomato paste, cinnamon, and caraway; cook 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes, soaked chickpeas, salt, and 2 liters water; simmer 1 hour.
Rinse freekeh and add to the pot; cook 30 minutes until tender.
Adjust salt and add hot water if too thick.
Stir in chopped mint and serve hot with lemon.
